Items.Description:
任何组织都依赖信息技术 (IT) 进行运营，并且可能还依赖信息技术 (IT) 来创建和提供其产品。 这是一笔可观的开支。 将 IT 资源迁移到云端可以节省成本。

但是，必须仔细考虑和计划迁移到云。 为云迁移创建业务案例有助于促进财务团队和其他业务领域的支持。 它还可以帮助加速云迁移并提高业务敏捷性。

业务案例提供了您环境的技术和财务时间表的视图。 制定业务案例包括制定财务计划，该计划将技术考虑因素考虑在内并与业务成果保持一致。

商业案例的关键组成部分

规划业务案例时需要考虑几个关键组成部分。

环境范围：在构建环境的本地视图时，请考虑您的环境范围（从技术和财务角度）如何保持一致。 您希望确保您用于计划的技术环境与财务数据相匹配。

基线财务数据：财务数据的常见问题

  • 今天运行我的环境需要多少费用？
  • 我平均每年在服务器上花费多少？
  • 我在数据中心运营类别中的支出是多少，例如电力或租赁成本？
  • 下一次硬件更新是什么时候？

本地成本方案：如果您不迁移到云，请预测您的本地成本。

本地成本的 Azure 方案：在 Azure 方案中迁移到云时预测您的本地成本。 将您的环境转移到云中需要资源和时间，因此在业务案例中考虑到它们很重要。 包括云提供的所有核心优势。

迁移时间表和 Azure 成本：预测给定环境的迁移时间表和估计成本。 考虑如何优化和充分利用 Azure 投资。 例如，使用预留实例、上下扩展容量、使用 Azure 混合优势以及调整资源大小。

业务案例不仅仅是即时视图。 这是一个涵盖一段时间的计划。 当您转向云时，您的成本开始下降。 您可以预测与云迁移计划相关的本地支出随时间的减少。

一旦确定了本地工作负载和成本结构，您就可以构建优化的 Azure 消费计划。

作为最后一步，将云环境与本地或现状场景进行比较，以便您可以评估迁移到云的好处。 云储蓄

将资源迁移到云端可以为组织节省资金。 云计费模型不同于内部部署，创造了有意义的节省机会以降低成本。 然后可以将节省的资金重新投资到新技术计划中。

云成本是灵活的，可以通过以下方式降低：

你可以在 Azure 上使用启用了本地软件保障的 Windows Server 和 SQL Server 许可证。 此权益也适用于 RedHat 和 SUSE Linux 订阅。

例如，高性能计算场景、批处理作业、视觉渲染应用程序、包括持续集成和持续交付工作负载的开发/测试环境，或大规模无状态应用程序。

预留：提前预留资源可享受工作负载折扣。 作为回报，Microsoft 会将节省的费用作为高达 72% 的折扣转嫁给您。

用于计算的 Azure 储蓄计划：用于计算的 Azure 储蓄计划是一种灵活的成本节约计划，通过一年或三年的合同，可以在现收现付价格上节省大量资金。 符合条件的计算服务包括虚拟机、专用主机、容器实例、Azure 高级功能和 Azure 应用服务。 无论区域、实例大小或操作系统如何，节省都适用于这些计算服务。 为了进一步优化成本和灵活性，可以将 Azure 储蓄计划与 Azure 预订相结合。 有关详细信息，请参阅 Azure 储蓄计划概述和 Azure 储蓄计划文档。

它包括 Azure 虚拟机的 Microsoft 软件费用和其他服务的特殊开发/测试定价。

扩展安全更新：获得对 SQL Server 2008 和 SQL Server 2008 R2 的持续支持，即使它们的支持生命周期已经结束。 你可以将本地 SQL Server 实例迁移到 Azure 虚拟机、Azure SQL 数据库，或者留在本地并购买扩展安全更新。 通过迁移到 Azure VM，您可以获得免费的扩展安全补丁。

工具和计算器

您可以使用许多有价值的工具和计算器来帮助准备云迁移的业务案例。

您可以输入本地基础设施的详细信息，包括服务器、数据库、存储和网络、许可假设和成本。 计算器从 Azure 服务中创建匹配项以创建高级初始 TCO 比较。 但是，需要谨慎考虑 TCO 计算器的结果，因为本地服务器列表通常很复杂，在考虑 Azure 时可以采取优化步骤。

以前，检索 Azure 服务价格的唯一方法是使用 Azure 定价计算器或使用 Azure 门户。 此 API 为你提供未经身份验证的体验，以获取所有 Azure 服务的零售价。 使用 API 了解 Azure 服务针对不同地区和不同 SKU 的价格。 编程 API 还可以帮助您创建自己的工具，用于跨 SKU 和区域的内部分析和价格比较。

合作伙伴工具集： Microsoft 合作伙伴在 Azure Marketplace 中拥有可帮助创建迁移成本分析的工具。

结论

从本地迁移到 Azure 云可以在可扩展性、灵活性、成本节约、安全性和对尖端技术的访问方面为企业提供巨大优势。 适当的规划、测试和执行，以及解决潜在的挑战，对于成功迁移至关重要。 利用范围广泛的 Azure 服务可以使企业能够在动态和不断发展的数字环境中推动创新、提高生产力并实现其战略目标。



原文:

Any organization depends on information technology (IT) for its operations, and probably for creating and supplying its products as well. It's a significant expense. Migrating IT resources to the cloud offers the potential for cost savings.

However, a move to the cloud must be carefully considered and planned. Creating a business case for cloud migration can help foster support from your Finance team and other areas of the business. It can also help accelerate cloud migration and promote business agility.

A business case provides a view of the technical and financial timeline of your environment. Developing a business case includes building a financial plan that takes technical considerations into account and aligns with business outcomes.

Key components of a business case

There are several key components to consider when you plan a business case.

Environment scope: As you build out the on-premises view of your environment, think about how your environment scope—from both a technical and financial perspective—is aligned. You want to be sure the technical environment you're using for your plan matches up to the financial data.

Baseline financial data: Common questions for the financial data

  • How much does it cost to run my environment today?
  • What am I spending on servers in an average year?
  • What am I spending in my data center operations categories, for example, power or lease costs?
  • When is the next hardware refresh?

On-premises cost scenario: Forecast your on-premises costs if you don't migrate to the cloud.

Azure scenario for on-premises costs: Forecast your on-premises costs when you migrate to the cloud in an Azure scenario. It takes resources and time to shift your environment to the cloud, so it's important to account for them in the business case. Include all of the core benefits that the cloud provides.

Migration timeline and Azure costs: Forecast the migration timeline and estimated costs for a given environment. Consider how you can optimize and get the most out of your Azure investment. For example, use reserved instances, scale capacity up and down, use the Azure hybrid benefit, and right-size your resources.

A business case isn't just a moment-in-time view. It's a plan that covers a period of time. As you shift to the cloud, your costs begin to decrease. You can forecast the ramp-down in on-premises spending over time associated with your cloud migration plan.

Once on-premises workloads and cost structure have been identified, you can then build out your optimized Azure consumption plan.

As a final step, compare the cloud environment to an on-premises or status quo scenario so you can assess the benefit of migrating to the cloud. The Azure view will show reduced on-premises costs over time, your Azure environment costs, and any costs associated with the cloud migration.

Cloud savings

Migrating resources to the cloud can save organizations money. Cloud billing models differ from on-premises, creating meaningful savings opportunities to reduce costs. Savings can then be reinvested into new technology initiatives.

Cloud costs are flexible, and they can be reduced with:

Azure hybrid benefit: Reduce the costs of running workloads in the cloud by using this licensing benefit. You can use your on-premises Software Assurance-enabled Windows Server and SQL Server licenses on Azure. This benefit also applies to RedHat and SUSE Linux subscriptions.

Spot virtual machines: You can use spot virtual machines with deep discounts for workloads that can be interrupted and don't need to complete within a specific time frame. For example, high-performance computing scenarios, batch processing jobs, visual rendering applications, dev/test environments including continuous integration and continuous delivery workloads, or large-scale stateless applications.

Reservations: Receive a discount on your workloads when you reserve resources in advance. In return, Microsoft passes the savings onto you as discounts of up to 72 percent.

Azure savings plan for compute: Azure savings plan for compute is a flexible cost-saving plan that generates significant savings on pay-as-you-go prices with a one-year or three-year contract. Eligible compute services include virtual machines, dedicated hosts, container instances, Azure premium functions, and Azure app services. Savings apply to these compute services regardless of the region, instance size, or operating system. To further optimize cost and flexibility, you can combine an Azure savings plan with Azure Reservations. For more information, see Azure savings plan overview and Azure savings plan documentation.

Azure dev/test pricing: Take advantage of discounted rates for development and testing. It includes the Microsoft software charges on Azure Virtual Machines and special dev/test pricing on other services.

Extended security updates: Receive continued support for SQL Server 2008 and SQL Server 2008 R2 even though they've reached the end of their support lifecycle. You can migrate your on-premises SQL Server instances to Azure Virtual Machines, Azure SQL Database, or stay on-premises and purchase extended security updates. You receive free extended security patches by migrating to an Azure VM.

Tools and calculators

There are many valuable tools and calculators you can use to help prepare a business case for your cloud migration.

Azure Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator: Use the TCO calculator to estimate the cost savings you can realize when you migrate your workloads to Azure.

You can enter details of your on-premises infrastructure, including servers, databases, storage, and networking, licensing assumptions and costs. The calculator creates a match from Azure Services to create a high-level initial TCO comparison. However, the results of the TCO calculator need to be considered with care, since an on-premises server list is often complex and optimization steps can be taken when considering Azure.

Retail Rates Prices API: Use the Retail Rates Prices API to retrieve retail prices for all Azure services. Previously, the only way that you could retrieve prices for Azure services was to either use the Azure Pricing Calculator or use the Azure portal. This API gives you an unauthenticated experience to get retail rates for all Azure services. Use the API to explore prices for Azure services against different regions and different SKUs. The programmatic API can also help you create your own tools for internal analysis and price comparison across SKUs and regions.

Azure VM cost estimator: This Power BI model allows you to estimate your cost savings against pay-as-you-go pricing by optimizing Azure offers and benefits for VMs like Azure Hybrid Benefit and reserved instances.

Azure Pricing Calculator: Use the pricing calculator to estimate your hourly or monthly costs for Azure products.

Partner toolsets: Microsoft Partners have tools in the Azure Marketplace that can help create a migration cost analysis.

Conclusion

Migrating from on-premises to Azure cloud can offer substantial advantages to businesses in terms of scalability, flexibility, cost savings, security, and access to cutting-edge technologies. Proper planning, testing, and execution, along with addressing potential challenges, are crucial for a successful migration. Leveraging the wide range of Azure services can empower businesses to drive innovation, enhance productivity, and achieve their strategic goals in the dynamic and ever-evolving digital landscape.

Items.Title: 如何创建EC2实例（Microsoft Windows Server 2022 base） | How to Create EC2 Instance (Microsoft Windows Server 2022 base)
Items.Description:
奇里贝特

发表于 4 月 8 日

它是现代商业运作的重要组成部分。

无论您来这里是为了研究、学习还是其他目的，本文都将教您如何在 Microsoft Windows 10 上创建 Amazon Web Service (AWS) EC2 或虚拟机，以及如何终止它以避免不必要的计费.

文章将分为三个部分。

甲部
如何创建 EC2 实例

步骤 1. 任何其他流程之前的第一步是创建并登录您的 AWS 账户。 如果您还没有帐户，可以在此处免费创建一个，然后按照提示操作。

第 2 步：导航至 AWS 管理控制台或在搜索栏中搜索 EC2 并打开仪表板。

第 3 步。下图是您的仪表板的外观。 您可以看到有 0 个实例在运行，因为您还没有创建任何实例。 现在，单击启动实例。 第 4 步：您需要为要创建的实例选择一个名称。 我选择我的作为（实例）。 您可以选择您的名字或任何其他喜欢的词。 此外，在右侧，您可以选择要创建的实例数。 现在，我们只创建 1。 第 5 步：此步骤需要选择您的 AMI（Amazon Machine Image）。 这包含您的操作系统。 我们正在 Windows 10 上创建虚拟机，因此，选择 Microsoft Windows。 第六步：这是非常重要的一步。 您需要为您的实例创建一个唯一的密钥对。 稍后您将需要它来登录。 点击“Create new key pair” 第 7 步：为您的密钥对选择一个唯一的名称（以前未使用过的名称）。 我选择我的作为维克。 我还选择了 .pem 文件，因为 .ppk 文件无法解密密码。 现在，单击创建密钥对。 第 8 步：此处，密钥对已创建并自动下载。 如何连接 EC2 实例

这部分涉及创建实例或虚拟机后连接实例或虚拟机所需的必要步骤。

第 1 步：返回您的实例。 单击它并选择屏幕右上角的“连接”。 下载远程桌面文件（RDP 文件）。 您应该会在屏幕下方看到它已下载（以红色标记）。 现在，单击获取密码。 第 3 步：要获取登录密码，您需要在启动实例时创建的密钥对。 请记住它是“Vick.pem”。 点击“上传私钥对”。 选择 .pem 文件并打开。 第 4 步：下图是您的私钥内容应该是什么样子的。 它必须以“-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----”开头。 然后，单击解密密码。 第 5 步：现在，这些是您的登录详细信息。 公共 DNS、用户名和密码。 将它们复制出来并保存在某个地方。 第 6 步：单击已下载的 RDP 文件。 它在屏幕下方以红色标记为第 7 步：这就是您的远程桌面连接的外观。 单击连接 第 8 步：输入您保存在某处的密码，然后单击确定。 第9步：我们开始吧！ 虚拟机已连接成功。 单击“x”按钮离开界面。 如何终止 EC2 实例

这部分涉及终止实例所需的步骤，以避免不必要的计费。

第 1 步：返回您的 EC2 仪表板并单击实例。 您现在应该有 1 个实例正在运行。 第二步：选择实例，点击“实例状态”。 从下拉选项中，选择终止实例。 第 3 步：确认您要终止。 第四步：实例状态将从“正在运行”变为“正在关闭”。 第五步：此时，实例状态将再次从“正​​在关闭”变为“已终止”。

恭喜！ 您刚刚在 Windows 10 上创建、连接并终止了 AWS EC2 或虚拟机。

一旦解除暂停，eddahchiribett 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

如果 eddahchiribett 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

原文:

Chiribett

Posted on Apr 8

AWS EC2 (Elastic Compute 2) is one of the numerous services run and offered by Amazon. It is an essential part of modern business operations.

Whether you're here for some research, study or other purposes, this article will teach and show you how to create an Amazon Web Service (AWS) EC2 or a virtual machine on Microsoft Windows 10 and also how to terminate it to avoid unnecessary billing.

The article will be divided into three parts.

Part A: Creating an EC2 Instance Part B: Connecting the Instance and

Part C: Terminating the EC2 Instance.

Part A
How to Create an EC2 Instance

Step 1. The first step before any other process is to create and Login to your AWS account. You can create a free one here if you do not already have an account and then follow the prompt.

Step 2. Navigate to the AWS Management Console or simply search for EC2 in the search bar and open the dashboard.

Step 3. The image below is what your dashboard should look like. You can see you have 0 instances running because you haven't created any. Now, click on Launch Instance. Step 4: You need to choose a name for the instance you want to create. I chose mine as (Instance). You can pick your name or any other preferred word. Also, at the right hand side, you can choose the number of instances you want to create. Now, we are creating just 1. Step 5: This step requires picking your AMI (Amazon Machine Image). This contains your operating system. (Linux, Ubuntu, Windows, Mac etc). We are creating a virtual machine on Windows 10, so, select Microsoft Windows. Step 6: This is a very important step. You need to create a unique key pair for your instance. You will need this to login later on. Click on "Create new key pair" Step 7: Choose a unique name for your key pair (A name that has not been used before). I chose mine as Vick. I also selected the .pem file because the .ppk file could not decrypt the password. Now, click on Create Key Pair. Step 8: Here, the Key Pair has been created and automatically downloaded. (Take a look at the file highlighted in red below the screen) Step 9: You will get a notification that your instance has been launched successfully. Part B

How to Connect an EC2 Instance

This part involves the necessary steps needed to connect your instance or virtual machine after it has been created.

Step 1: Go back to your instance. Click on it and select Connect at the top right of the screen. (Marked in red) Step 2: Connect using RDP. Download the Remote Desktop File (RDP File). You should see it downloaded below your screen (Marked in red). Now, click on get password. Step 3: To get the login password, you need the key pair that was created when you launched your instance. Remember it was “Vick.pem”. Click on “upload private key pair”. Select the .pem file and open. Step 4: The image below is what your private key contents should look like. It MUST begin with “——-BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY——-”. Then, click on Decrypt password. Step 5: Now, these are your login details. The Public DNS, Username and password. Copy them out and save somewhere. Step 6: Click on the RDP file that was downloaded. It is marked in red below the screen Step 7: This is what your Remote Desktop Connection should look like. Click on connect Step 8: Enter the password that you saved somewhere and click ok. Step 9: Here we go! The virtual machine has been connected successfully. Click the “x” button to leave the interface. Part C

How to Terminate an EC2 Instance

This part involves the steps needed to terminate the instance in order to avoid unnecessary billing.

Step 1: Go back to your EC2 Dashboard and click Instances. You should have 1 instance running now. Step 2: Select the instance and click “Instance State”. (Marked in red). From the drop down options, choose terminate instance. Step 3: Confirm you want to terminate. Step 4: The instance status will change from “running” to “shutting down”. Step 5: Now, the instance status will change again from “shutting down” to “terminated”.

Congratulations! You just created, connected and terminated an AWS EC2 or Virtual Machine on Windows 10.

Items.Title: 如何使用 AWS 管理控制台设置 Windows 虚拟机 | How to Setup A Windows Virtual Machine using AWS Management Console
Items.Description:
莫伊诺卢瓦·阿德努加

发表于 4 月 8 日

设置虚拟机是了解云时首先要学习的技能之一。 它非常重要，因为它是云计算的基础。 虚拟机或服务器通常称为实例，它们可以互换使用。 先决条件：您应该有一个 AWS 账户或在此处注册以创建一个账户。

启动实例

  1. 登录 AWS 控制台后，搜索并选择 EC2。

  2. 在 EC2 控制台仪表板中，从 Launch Instance 下拉列表中选择Launch Instance以创建虚拟服务器。

  3. 输入实例的描述性名称。

  4. 在Application and OS Images (Amazon Machine Image)下，选择 Windows，因为这是我们为实例设置的所需操作系统 (OS)。
    对于Amazon Machine Image (AMI) ，我们可以坚持使用符合免费套餐条件的默认设置。 实例类型通常用于设置实例的硬件配置，如存储容量、存储类型（ssd 或 hdd）、CPU、内存或网络性能。 我们会将其保留为默认值。

  5. 在Key pair下，创建一个密钥对或使用以前创建的密钥对。 这允许与实例的安全连接，并且是不应跳过的步骤，因为没有它就无法连接到实例。 创建密钥对时会下载一个文件。

  6. 保留其他配置的默认选择，查看配置摘要并启动实例。

  7. 成功启动后，我们可以在 EC2 仪表板上看到它正在运行。 单击实例可查看实例的更多信息。

连接到已启动的实例

  1. 选择实例并单击连接按钮

  2. 导航到RDP 客户端选项卡并下载远程桌面文件。

  3. 通过上传密钥对文件生成密码并解密密码。 复制生成的密码，因为此密码将用于连接远程客户端。

  4. 使用合适的应用程序打开远程桌面文件。 我使用了 App Store 中的 Microsoft Remote Desktop App。 输入第3步生成的密码，虚拟实例启动。

收到此提示后，单击继续


也可以随意执行一些操作。 我浏览并确认存储空间为 30GB。

终止实例

一旦完成一个实例，它应该被终止，因为不需要的资源不应该继续运行，因为它会产生成本。

  1. 选择实例并在Instance state下拉列表中选择Terminate instance 。

  2. 在提示确认时选择终止。

原文:

Moyinoluwa Adenuga

Posted on Apr 8

Setting up a virtual machine is one of the first skills learned when learning about the cloud. It is very important as it is the foundation of cloud computing. A virtual machine or server is usually referred to as an instance and they are used interchangeably. Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) is the service which provides scalable computing capacity in the Amazon Web Services (AWS) Cloud. Amazon EC2 is used to launch as many or as few virtual servers as needed, configure security and networking, and manage storage.

Prerequisites: You should have an AWS account or register here to create an account.

Launching An Instance

  1. When signed in to the AWS console, search and select EC2.

  2. From the EC2 console dashboard, select the Launch Instance from the Launch Instance Dropdown to create a virtual server.

  3. Enter a descriptive name for the instance.

  4. Under Application and OS Images (Amazon Machine Image), select Windows as this is the desired operating system (OS) we are setting up for the instance.
    For the Amazon Machine Image (AMI), we can stick with the default which is Free tier eligible. An Amazon Machine Image (AMI) is a basic configuration that serves as a template for the instance.

  5. The instance type is usually used to set the hardware configurations of the instance such as storage capacity, storage type(ssd or hdd), CPU, memory or network performance. We would just leave this as default.

  6. Under Key pair, create a key pair or use a previously created one. This allows a secure connection to the instance and is a step that should not be skipped as one would not be able to connect to the instance without it. A file gets downloaded on creation of the key pair.

  7. Keep the default selections for other configurations, review the summary of the configurations and launch the instance.

  8. Once successfully launched, we are able to see it running on the EC2 dashboard. More information of the instance can be viewed by clicking it.

Connecting to the Launched Instance

  1. Select the instance and click the connect button

  2. Navigate to the RDP Client tab and download Remote desktop file.

  3. Generate password by uploading the key pair file and decrypt password. Copy the generated password as this password would be used to connect with the remote client.

  4. Open the Remote Desktop file with a suitable application. I used the Microsoft Remote Desktop App from App Store. Enter the password generated in step 3 and the virtual instance is started.

On getting this prompt, click continue


Feel free to also perform some actions. I browsed and also confirmed the storage was 30gb.

Terminating the Instance

Once done with an instance, it should be terminated as resources not needed should not be left running as it incurs costs.

  1. Select the instance and Choose Terminate instance in the Instance state dropdown.

  2. Choose Terminate when prompted for confirmation.

After the instance is terminated, it remains visible on the console for a short while before being removed from the console.

Items.Description:
大家好！

祝那些庆祝的复活节周末快乐！ 无论是家庭时间、工作，还是用自己体重的巧克力填满你的脸——让我们花点时间回顾一下过去一周。

像往常一样在 Twitter-verse 中进行很多事情，相当多的 Vercel/Next.JS 新闻，以及更多有趣的服务器组件游戏。

我们跳进去好吗？

他现在更新了他的 simple-rsc 存储库（与 Dan 自己合着）以拥有一个新的 README 并注释了所有源代码以帮助解释正在发生的事情。 如果您想了解幕后发生的事情，这将非常有用。

好吧，simple-rsc 变得更简单了。 用有用的注释对所有源代码进行注释，以了解它是如何工作的，并添加了一个自述文件以供入门❤️试用 React 服务器组件！

关于使用 Midjourney 制作网络矢量图形，Meng 提供了一个非常酷的提示。 我喜欢这个想法，因为这通常是一个问题，作为前端开发人员，你会有一个好主意，但它需要一些插图或矢量图才能真正实现。 我很难跟上并不断学习 JS，而无需在我的盘子中添加图形设计 😀这是一个超级好的捷径！

中途 👉 矢量 👉 网页设计

服务器组件即将推出 Bun v0.6.0

真的很喜欢他们撰写文章并遵循思路的方式。 很少阅读和出色的结果。

一些不错的 Array 函数在第 4 阶段，并且已经随 Chrome 110 一起提供。

如果您有兴趣试一试 Tailwind，这可能值得一试。

我在推广我的免费@ProTailwind教程方面做得不够好。我花了*很多个月*来研究这些，并且它们可供注册邮件列表的任何人使用。

具有我标志性教学风格的高级@tailwindcss课程，免费👀

因此，只需访问您的服务器一次，您就可以触发数据库更新并取回更新后的 UI。 这是我对 RSC 感到兴奋的最令人信服的原因之一。

检查一下，一个服务器计数器：

本周 CSS 选择器嵌套已添加到 Chrome Beta 中！ 缓慢但肯定地将它推向黄金时段。

超越 Sass！ 没有样式，并且建立在 React Aria 钩子之上，如果您喜欢可组合和可定制的类型（而不是像 Mantine 或 MUI 那样现成的），这看起来像是一个简洁的设计系统。

我们刚刚发布了 React Aria Components 的第一个 alpha 版本，这是一个建立在 React Aria hooks 之上的全新无样式组件库！ 我目前无法获得足够的 Zod，因为我正在努力将它添加到我们目前工作的更多生产代码中。 如此简洁的库，Vercel 只是将其添加到 Next 中以确保路由器类型安全。 惊人的。

等等，他们把 Zod 放在了下一个？ 说到 Next.JS，本周发布了 13.3 版，其中包含一些很酷的新功能。 我迫不及待地想试用基于文件的元数据 API 和动态 OG 图像。 本周反应

我的TWIR首选：

我💯同意对 Vercel/Next.JS 的看法以及它带来的炒作和兴奋。 我也同意“使用客户端”的名称，但也不太同意“使用交互”的名字 😀但幸运的是，这只是命名语义。 我认为像“使用浏览器”这样简单的事情也同样具有描述性。 不是它运行在服务器或客户端，而是它针对浏览器，Theo 的“交互性”。

我仍然没有巩固我对裁判的心智模型。 它们感觉像是黑魔法，我敢肯定我错过了很多有用的用例。 虽然我认为即使是个人的、快速的项目，我也更喜欢使用 TS，但看到其他做事的方式总是很有趣。 虽然 JSDocs 现在不适合我，但谁知道它是否会成为未来特定案例的良好解决方案。 在另一边见！



原文:

Hello folks!

Happy Easter weekend for those celebrating! Whether it's family time, work, or stuffing your face full with your own body weight in chocolate - let's take a moment to look back over the past week.

Lot going on in the Twitter-verse as usual, quite a bit of Vercel/Next.JS news, and more fun and games with server components.

Let's jump in shall we?

Ben was at it again with server components. He's now updated his simple-rsc repo (co-authored with Dan himself) to have a fresh README and annotated all source code to help explain what's happening. Super useful if you wanted to get an idea of what's going on under the hood.

Well, simple-rsc just got simpler. Annotated all source code with helpful comments to understand how it works, and added a README for getting started ❤️Take React Server Components for a spin!

github.com/bholmesdev/sim…

There was a very cool tip from Meng on using Midjourney for web vector graphics. I love this idea, as it's often the problem that as a frontend developer you'll have a great idea, but it needs some illustrations or vectors to really come to life. I have a hard enough time keeping up with and constantly learning JS without adding graphic design to my plate 😅 this is a super nice shortcut!

midjourney 👉 vector 👉 web design

Bun is going to have RSC support in v0.6.0

server components is coming in Bun v0.6.0

JP Camara blogs about their experience hunting down a performance bottleneck when using Tanstack Table, and ultimately, the 1000x speed improvements they gained with a one line change. Really love the way they set out the article and followed the train of thought. Great little read and a stellar result.

Some nice Array functions are in Stage 4,a nd already shipped with Chrome 110.

TIL:Array.toReversed, Array.toSorted, and more, are in Stage 4 and have shipped in Chrome 110.Reverse and sort arrays WITHOUT MUTATING THE ORIGINAL array!

github.com/tc39/proposal-…

Simon reminds us he has some free Tailwind content just by signing up to his mailing list. If you're interested in dipping your toes into Tailwind, this could be worth a quick punt.

I'm not doing a good enough job promoting my FREE @ProTailwind tutorials.Spent *many months* working on these and they're available to anyone who signs up for the mailing list.

Premium @tailwindcss courses with my signature teaching style, for FREE 👀

Lee released a new video detailing the new Next.JS App router (what I'm using with this blog!) which gives a nice look over the features in a quick 15 minute review.

Ben isn't the only one jumping into RSC's, vite had an update about their vite-rsc experiment which now supports mutations, very cool stuff.

Another update to the vite-rsc experiment with @cyco130.We got mutations working. So in one trip to your server, you can trigger a database update and get back the updated UI. This is one of the most compelling reasons I'm excited about RSC.

Check this out, a server counter:

CSS Selector nesting got added to Chrome Beta this week! Slowly but surely moving it's way to the prime time.

Move over Sass! CSS Selector Nesting dropped in Chrome Stable today.

The Adobe React Spectrum team dropped a brand new design system, React Aria Components. Unstyled, and built on top of React Aria hooks, this looks like a neat design system if you like the composable and customizable kind (rather than off the shelf such as Mantine or MUI).

We just shipped the first alpha of React Aria Components, a brand new unstyled component library built on top of the React Aria hooks! 🚀It abstracts away glue code and provides a much simpler way to quickly build accessible components for your design.

react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/rea…

Zod, more Zod. I can't get enough of Zod at the moment as I'm working on adding it to more of our production code at work at the moment. Such a neat library, and Vercel just added it into Next for router type safety. Amazing.

Wait wtf they put Zod in Next? 👀 github.com/vercel/next.js…

Speaking of Next.JS, version 13.3 was released this week with a few new cool features. I can't wait to try out the File Based Metadata API and Dynamic OG Images. Lot of buzz on Twitter for the Parallel Routes and Interception too.

This Week In React

My top picks for TWIR:

Theo's recap about the Twitter spaces with Dan, Ryan, and a bunch of other clever people, is a great watch. I 💯 agree with the take on Vercel/Next.JS and the hype and excitement that it brings. I also agree with the 'use client' name, but not so much 'use interactive' either 😅 but, luckily, this is just naming semantics. I think something as simple as 'use browser' could be equally as descriptive. It's not that it runs in server or client, but it's targeting the browser, Theo's 'interactivity'.

I still haven't solidified my mental model on refs. They feel like the dark arts, and I'm sure I'm missing a lot of useful use cases. This article from Nadia was a good read, that I'm sure I'll need to refer back to again!

Jim writes about types in JS files by using JSDoc instead of TypeScript. While I think even for personal, quick projects, I'd much prefer to use TS, it's always interesting to see other ways of doing things. And while the JSDocs don't float my boat right now, who knows if it could be a good solution for a particular case in the future. (and hey, more zod!)

CSS Weekly is back but we're out of time for this week. See you on the other side!

这个演示展示了我们如何使用预制的机器学习解决方案来识别您希望呈现给它的任何图像上的对象（是的，一次不止一个！）。 更好的是，我们不仅知道图像包含一个对象，而且我们还可以获得它找到的每个对象的边界框坐标，这使您可以在图像中突出显示找到的对象。

This demo shows how we can use a pre made machine learning solution to recognize objects (yes, more than one at a time!) on any image you wish to present to it. Even better, not only do we know that the image contains an object, but we can also get the co-ordinates of the bounding box for each object it finds, which allows you to highlight the found object in the image.

For this demo we are loading a model using the ImageNet-SSD architecture, to recognize 90 common objects it has already been taught to find from the COCO dataset.

If what you want to recognize is in that list of things it knows about (for example a cat, dog, etc), this may be useful to you as is in your own projects, or just to experiment with Machine Learning in the browser and get familiar with the possibilities of machine learning.
